docs.title: Home
docs.text: Welcome to my little place where you can find content I have created.

docs.title: Biography
docs.text: Power Apps. Power Automate. Microsoft 365. Azure. Daniel works with a lot of different technologies. He loves low-code AND code. Daniel is a Power Platform Developer Advocate at Microsoft, where he continues to enthuse people about the possibilities of the Power Platform. Daniel loves to talk to you about what the Power Platform can do for you, whether you're a developer, an IT Pro, an architect, or an administrator.

docs.title: What you'll find here
docs.text:
Events — Upcoming and past events where I'm speaking or have spoken.

Sessions — Resources and links for my conference sessions, including Copilot Studio and Copilot Cowork.

Projects — Open-source projects I'm working on, like Power Platform Connectors and Cowork Plugins.

docs.title: Privacy policy
docs.text:
Last updated: June 15, 2026

This is my personal website. I want to be upfront about what happens when you visit.

docs.title: What I collect
docs.text: I use cookies to remember your preferences (like dark mode or cookie consent) and to understand how people use the site. That's it. I don't collect personal information, email addresses, or anything I could use to identify you.

docs.title: Analytics
docs.text: I use anonymous analytics to see which pages get visited and how people find the site. This helps me figure out what content is useful. The analytics don't track you across other websites and don't collect personal data.

docs.title: Cookies
docs.text:
The site uses a small number of cookies:

  • Consent cookie - remembers whether you accepted or declined cookies
  • Analytics cookies - only set if you consent, used for anonymous usage stats

You can change your cookie preferences at any time by clearing your browser cookies and revisiting the site.

docs.title: Third-party services
docs.text: The site is hosted on GitHub Pages. GitHub may collect technical information (like IP addresses) as part of serving the site. See GitHub's privacy statement for details.

docs.title: Questions
docs.text: If you have questions about this, reach out to me on LinkedIn or Twitter.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text: I like building things that make my work (and hopefully yours) easier. Here's what I'm working on.

docs.title: Cowork Plugins
docs.text:
Copilot Cowork is a way to work with Copilot in Microsoft 365 on longer, multi-step tasks. You can extend it with plugins that connect to MCP servers and add custom skills.

I've been building a few of these. Grab them if they're useful, or steal the structure to make your own.

docs.title: Learn for Copilot Cowork
docs.text:
This plugin hooks into the Microsoft Learn MCP Server so Cowork can search through Microsoft docs and pull together research for you.

It has two skills:

  • Learn Deck - researches a topic on Microsoft Learn and builds a PowerPoint with speaker notes and sources
  • Learn One-Pager - same idea, but outputs a one-page Word doc instead

🌎 Learn for Copilot Cowork (GitHub)

docs.title: Power Platform Connectors
docs.text: I spend a lot of time building connectors for Power Platform. This section has some resources that might save you time if you're doing the same.

docs.title: VS Code Extension
docs.text:
I made a VS Code extension with snippets for building custom connectors. Type ppc after hitting ctrl+space (Windows) or command+space (Mac) and you'll see what's available.

🌎 Power Platform Connectors (VS Code Extension)

docs.title: Independent Publisher Connectors
docs.text:
Before 2021, only service owners could publish connectors. If you wanted a Tesla connector, Tesla had to build it.

The Independent Publisher Connector program changed that. Now anyone can build and publish a connector for any public API. The repo below is where all of them live.

🌎 Power Platform Connectors GitHub Repository

docs.title: Terms of use
docs.text:
Last updated: June 15, 2026

This is a personal website. There's not much to say here, but for completeness:

docs.title: Content
docs.text: Everything on this site is my own opinion and doesn't represent my employer. I try to keep things accurate, but I make no guarantees. If you find something wrong, let me know and I'll fix it.

docs.title: Links
docs.text: I link to external sites for convenience. I'm not responsible for their content or availability.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: You're welcome to share links to this site. If you want to quote or reference my content, please attribute it. Don't scrape the site or republish large portions without asking.

docs.title: Questions
docs.text: If you have questions, reach out to me on LinkedIn or Twitter.
